content.problemStatement:
Chef likes assigning scores to pairs of numbers in an unusual way:


Consider a pair (X,Y)(X, Y)(X,Y) of integers in decimal representation. If they don't have the same number of digits, add leading zeros to the smaller of these integers until they have the same number of digits. Let's denote the number of digits in each of the resulting decimal representations by DDD.
Reorder the digits of YYY in an arbitrary way.
For each iii (1ŌēżiŌēżD1 \le i \le D1ŌēżiŌēżD), calculate the absolute difference between the iii-th digit of XXX and the iii-th digit of YYY, and sum up these differences.
The score of the pair (X,Y)(X, Y)(X,Y) is the minimum possible value of this sum.
Consider the pair (3178,10920)(3178, 10920)(3178,10920) as an example. The scoring procedure would be:


Add a leading zero to XXX, so the decimal representations are "03178" and "10920".
Reorder "10920" e.g. to "01029".
The score is ŌłŻ0ŌłÆ0ŌłŻ+ŌłŻ3ŌłÆ1ŌłŻ+ŌłŻ1ŌłÆ0ŌłŻ+ŌłŻ7ŌłÆ2ŌłŻ+ŌłŻ8ŌłÆ9ŌłŻ=9|0-0| + |3-1| + |1-0| + |7-2| + |8-9| = 9ŌłŻ0ŌłÆ0ŌłŻ+ŌłŻ3ŌłÆ1ŌłŻ+ŌłŻ1ŌłÆ0ŌłŻ+ŌłŻ7ŌłÆ2ŌłŻ+ŌłŻ8ŌłÆ9ŌłŻ=9, since it is impossible to achieve a smaller sum.
Now, Chef has a range [L,R][L, R][L,R], and he wants to calculate the sum of scores of all pairs of integers in this range ŌĆĢ formally, Ōłæi=LRŌłæj=LRscore(i,j)\sum_{i=L}^{R} \sum_{j=L}^{R} \mathrm{score}(i, j)Ōłæi=LRŌĆŗŌłæj=LRŌĆŗscore(i,j). Since he is very busy with all his problem cooking, he asks you to write a program that would compute this sum. Since it could be very large, compute it modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers LLL and RRR.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ŌĆĢ the sum of scores modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007.
